chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.S- 1783 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For Katcha Peshi. 17.11.2016. Syed Inayat Hussain Shah, Advocate for the petitioner alongwith petitioner. Mr. Ali Abbas Memon, State Counsel alongwith SIP Muhammad Changal on bhelaf of SSP Hyderabad, SIP Dhani Bux on behalf of SHO Chalgari. -.-.-. Today, Sate Counsel has filed parawise reply and according to which police officials have stated that they are not siding with private respondents. However, this factual aspect has been controverted by petitioner’s Counsel by further submitting that private respondents are causing continuous harassment to the petitioner and so much so petitioner is finding difficulty to pursue the cases filed by him against the private respondents on account of threats. This position has been categorically refuted by Mr. Imtiaz Ali Abbasi, Advocate, who today has filed vakalatnama on behalf of private respondents, who are also present in Court. This petition is disposed of with the directions that police officials shall ensure that none of the parties; petitioner and private respondents should take law in their hands and in particular the private respondents are further restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioner. In this regard, police officials are directed to act strictly in accordance with law and must ensure that no law and order situation is created in their area and particularly arising out of the present dispute. The parties and especially police officials are put to further notice that disobedience of this order will be construed as a contumacious violation of Court’s order and appropriate proceedings shall be initiated against the delinquent party.

In this regard, police officials are directed to act strictly in accordance with law and must ensure that no law and order situation is created in their area and particularly arising out of the present dispute. The parties and especially police officials are put to further notice that disobedience of this order will be construed as a contumacious violation of Court’s order and appropriate proceedings shall be initiated against the delinquent party. Copy of this order be provided to learned State Counsel for compliance. JUDGE
